issue.title: Tiki Doctor (backend): tiki_doctor command — surface .tiki workspace health (state, release drift, reconciler hook)

successCriteria.description: tiki_doctor returns a DiagnosticsReport for a valid .tiki/ directory without error
An archive/ release whose JSON says status:"active" is reported with archivedButActive: true
A recentReleases entry with no matching JSON file appears in recentReleasesMissingJson
reconcilerHookInstalled is true when a Stop/SubagentStop hook runs reconcile-state.mjs, false otherwise
Command registered in invoke_handler; cargo test --lib and cargo clippy -- --deny warnings are clean

phases.title: DiagnosticsReport types + command shell (framework/state checks) + register
phases.content:
Foundation phase. In apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/state.rs, add a DiagnosticsReport struct and a nested ReleaseCheck struct, both with #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] + #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] (uniform with every other struct in the file). Fields on DiagnosticsReport: framework_version: Option<String>, state_valid: bool, schema_version: Option<u32>, active_work_count: usize, release_checks: Vec<ReleaseCheck>, recent_releases_missing_json: Vec<String>, reconciler_hook_installed: bool. Fields on ReleaseCheck: version: String, location: String ("active" | "archive"), status: String, archived_but_active: bool.

In apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs, add the command: #[tauri::command] pub fn tiki_doctor(tiki_path: Option<String>) -> Result<DiagnosticsReport, String>. Resolve tiki_path defaulting to cwd/.tiki exactly like load_tiki_releases (commands.rs:232-238). In THIS phase, implement only: framework_version (reuse the read logic from read_framework_version, commands.rs:816 — read <tiki>/.framework-version, trim, None if absent), state_valid + schema_version + active_work_count (read <tiki>/state.json, parse into TikiState; state_valid = parse Ok, schema_version from the parsed field, active_work_count = activeWork.len(); on parse failure state_valid=false and the rest default). Leave release_checks = vec![], recent_releases_missing_json = vec![], reconciler_hook_installed = false as TODO placeholders to be filled in phases 2 and 3 (add a // TODO(phase 2)/(phase 3) comment at each).

Register tiki_doctor in the Tauri invoke_handler (generate_handler! macro) so the command is callable immediately — #262 depends on this. Add one #[cfg(test)] test asserting tiki_doctor returns Ok for a valid temp .tiki (use the existing temp-dir helpers in the tests module as a model). NOTE FOR PHASES 2/3: both will edit the tiki_doctor body (the placeholder lines) and the tests module — coordinate so edits don't collide.
verification: DiagnosticsReport + ReleaseCheck structs exist in state.rs with camelCase serde
cargo build -p tiki-desktop compiles (command returns a report with framework/state fields populated, release/hook fields stubbed)
Phase-1 test: tiki_doctor returns Ok for a valid temp .tiki
phases.summary: Added DiagnosticsReport + ReleaseCheck structs (state.rs, camelCase serde); implemented tiki_doctor command with framework_version + state_valid/schema_version/active_work_count live, release/hook fields stubbed (TODO phase 2/3); registered in lib.rs invoke_handler; SC1 test passes (cargo test --lib).

phases.title: Release consistency checks (releaseChecks + recentReleasesMissingJson)
phases.content:
Fill the release_checks and recent_releases_missing_json fields in tiki_doctor.

release_checks: scan <tiki>/releases/*.json (location "active") and <tiki>/releases/archive/*.json (location "archive"). For each, parse enough to read version + status (reuse the TikiRelease parse / read_release_dir pattern at commands.rs:329; a lightweight serde struct reading just {version, status} is also fine). Set archived_but_active = (location == "archive" && status == "active"). Push one ReleaseCheck per file. IMPORTANT (from review): in this repo every archived release legitimately keeps status:"active" (ship mv's without flipping status; #259 made location the source of truth), so archived_but_active will be true for ALL archived releases — that is correct/expected here. Compute it faithfully; whether it is a 'warning' is #262's concern, not this command's.

recent_releases_missing_json: read <tiki>/state.json history.recentReleases[].version, build the set of versions that have a JSON file in releases/ OR releases/archive/, and report versions present in recentReleases but absent from that set (sorted). This adapts check_release_json_parity (commands.rs:272) but compares state-history -> JSON instead of changelog -> JSON.

Add #[cfg(test)] tests: (SC2) build a temp .tiki with an archive/ release whose JSON says status:"active" and assert that ReleaseCheck has archived_but_active=true — the existing temp_tiki_with_releases helper (commands.rs:922) already creates exactly this fixture; reuse or extend it. (SC3) a temp state.json recentReleases entry with no matching JSON file appears in recent_releases_missing_json. Edits the tiki_doctor body + tests module — see Phase 1 note re: overlap with Phase 3.
verification: release_checks populated: one entry per release JSON across releases/ and releases/archive/ with correct location/status
archived_but_active true for an archive/ file with status:active (SC2 test passes)
recent_releases_missing_json reports recentReleases versions with no JSON file (SC3 test passes)
phases.summary: Filled release_checks (reuses load_tiki_releases with include_archived; per-file version/location/status/archived_but_active) and recent_releases_missing_json (new compute_recent_releases_missing_json: state history.recentReleases minus JSON files in releases/ + archive/). SC2 + SC3 tests pass.

phases.title: Reconciler-hook detection + final test/clippy gate
phases.content:
Fill the reconciler_hook_installed field. Promote the existing reconcile_groups test helper (commands.rs:846, currently inside #[cfg(test)]) into a real (non-test) function — e.g. fn reconciler_hook_installed(project: &Path) -> bool that reads <project>/.claude/settings.json, and returns true if any group under hooks.Stop OR hooks.SubagentStop has a hooks[].command containing "reconcile-state.mjs". Be robust to a missing/unparseable settings.json (return false, do not error). Note the tiki_path passed to tiki_doctor points at <project>/.tiki, so derive the project root (parent of tiki_path) to locate .claude/settings.json. Wire reconciler_hook_installed into the tiki_doctor report.

Add #[cfg(test)] tests for SC4: a temp project whose .claude/settings.json registers reconcile-state.mjs under a Stop hook -> reconciler_hook_installed true; a project with no such hook -> false. The existing temp_project()/read_settings() helpers model the fixture.

Final gate for SC5: run cargo test --lib (all green) and cargo clippy -- --deny warnings (clean). If the running desktop app holds an exe lock, use cargo test --lib (not the full bin build) per the known Windows lock gotcha. Edits the tiki_doctor body + tests module — see Phase 1 note re: overlap with Phase 2.
verification: reconciler_hook_installed function exists outside #[cfg(test)] and is wired into tiki_doctor
SC4 tests pass: true with a Stop/SubagentStop reconcile hook, false without
phases.summary: Promoted hook detection to real fn reconciler_hook_installed(project) (robust to missing/unparseable settings.json); wired into tiki_doctor via path.parent(). SC4 test covers Stop + SubagentStop + unrelated + missing. Final gate: cargo test --lib 52 passed; cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings clean.
